candidates.why: Captures recurring financing costs in a single line and avoids duplicate-label/double-count issues among underlying interest components.
Conceptually strong, but duplicated on the sheet and overlaps with lease-interest detail, creating double-count risk.

candidates.why: Recurring interest component, but only partial financing cost.
Recurring interest component excluding leases, but not available in all years.
May be recurring finance cost, but could include non-interest items; safer inside Financial expenses total.
Capitalized contra-expense, not a period finance cost to include directly.
Supplemental lease schedule item on balance sheet; not preferred over income statement finance expense lines.

variables.notes: Used the single finance-expense line because the statement contains overlapping and duplicated interest labels, which makes granular selection prone to double counting. Excludes net financial items because that would pull in FX/equity-accounted items.

candidates.why: Best available equity line. The first Total equity appears to exclude non-controlling interests because adding Non-controlling interests reconciles to total assets.

Total balance sheet, not equity.
